externalProblemAnchor.problemStatement: Gaia public catalog measurements provide externally inspectable astrometric outcomes where excess-noise or residual claims must beat magnitude, color, crowding, scan-law, and source-family rivals before candidate birth.
externalProblemAnchor.measuredTargetOutcome: cross-slice astrometric residual under source-quality perturbation after magnitude, color, crowding, scan-law, and catalog-family controls
externalProblemAnchor.knownBaselineOrPrior: magnitude, color, crowding, scan-law coverage, source-family membership, and catalog quality flags are strong simple rivals for astrometric residual signals
externalProblemAnchor.externalValueRationale: An astrometric residual matters only when it is tied to public Gaia measurements and recurs across independent sky/source slices beyond catalog-quality artifacts.
externalProblemAnchor.domainScientificSignificance: An astrophysics catalog residual has domain scientific significance only when it exposes a reproducible measurement-outcome pattern across independent public sky/source slices that is not absorbed by magnitude, color, crowding, scan-law, or catalog-quality rivals.
externalProblemAnchor.discoveryScoredOutcome: Discovery-scored evidence would be a narrow public-catalog residual claim that changes interpretation of a measured astrometric outcome with cross-slice support, not an astropy or astroquery pipeline success.

gates.message: HardSeed birth requires a real external problem anchor before generator evidence can count.
External problem anchor must cite a public-safe external source.
External problem anchor must define a measured target outcome.
External problem anchor must define the known baseline or prior rival state.
External problem anchor must explain domain scientific significance beyond pipeline, package, or tool capability.
External problem anchor must define a discovery-scored outcome that is not only operational pipeline success.
External problem anchor must bind at least two public-safe significance evidence refs into the evidence set.
External problem anchor must be inspectable through the evidence refs.
Generator-only signals are blocked before HardSeed birth.
Package-only or pipeline-contract-only signals are blocked before HardSeed birth.
Internally interesting but externally unanchored signals are blocked before HardSeed birth.
Source-family documented behavior is blocked before HardSeed birth.
Known or trivial behavior is blocked before HardSeed birth.

domainSignificanceHypothesis.statement: An astrophysics catalog residual has domain scientific significance only when it exposes a reproducible measurement-outcome pattern across independent public sky/source slices that is not absorbed by magnitude, color, crowding, scan-law, or catalog-quality rivals.
domainSignificanceHypothesis.expectedDomainChange: Discovery-scored evidence would be a narrow public-catalog residual claim that changes interpretation of a measured astrometric outcome with cross-slice support, not an astropy or astroquery pipeline success.
domainSignificanceHypothesis.noveltyDiscriminator: The claimed signal must be not documented outside known source-family behavior and must change interpretation of the external target outcome. Candidate prediction: the astrometric residual recurs across independent sky and source slices after magnitude color crowding scan-law and catalog-quality controls. Rival prediction: magnitude color crowding scan-law source-family or catalog-quality controls erase the residual.
domainSignificanceHypothesis.falsifier: The significance claim is falsified if source-family, metadata, simple baseline, or negative-control mechanisms erase the residual. magnitude color crowding scan-law source-family or catalog-quality controls erase the residual

gates.message: HardSeed birth requires an explicit pre-birth domain-significance hypothesis, not only a runtime residual.
The domain-significance hypothesis must state the scientific/formal/measurement significance being tested.
The hypothesis must state what external domain interpretation, formal boundary, or measurement conclusion would change.
The hypothesis must define a novelty discriminator against known/source-family mechanisms.
The hypothesis must define a falsifier before HardSeed birth.
The domain-significance hypothesis must bind at least two public-safe external evidence refs already present in the runtime evidence set.
The domain-significance hypothesis must be tested by runtime evidence before HardSeed birth.
The domain-significance hypothesis must be supported by runtime evidence before HardSeed birth.

output.measuredVariable: cross-slice recurrence of astrometric-excess residual after magnitude, color, sky-position, and source-density baselines

runtimeSourceBinding.reason: Generator runtime output metrics are bound to the loaded discovery-anchor source-cache artifact.

output.candidateMechanismPrediction: A source-quality perturbation can expose an astrometric residual that recurs across independent public sky/source slices after magnitude, color, crowding, scan-law, and catalog-family controls. Prediction frozen for gaia_astrometric_excess_significance_generator-target-01: the astrometric residual recurs across independent sky and source slices after magnitude color crowding scan-law and catalog-quality controls
output.rivalMechanismPrediction: Magnitude, color, crowding, scan-law coverage, source-family membership, or catalog-quality flags explain the apparent residual. Rival prediction for gaia_astrometric_excess_significance_generator-target-01: magnitude color crowding scan-law source-family or catalog-quality controls erase the residual

gates.message: HardSeed birth requires a real external problem anchor before generator evidence can count.
External problem anchor must cite a public-safe external source.
External problem anchor must define a measured target outcome.
External problem anchor must define the known baseline or prior rival state.
External problem anchor must explain domain scientific significance beyond pipeline, package, or tool capability.
External problem anchor must define a discovery-scored outcome that is not only operational pipeline success.
External problem anchor must bind at least two public-safe significance evidence refs into the evidence set.
External problem anchor must be inspectable through the evidence refs.
Generator-only signals are blocked before HardSeed birth.
Package-only or pipeline-contract-only signals are blocked before HardSeed birth.
Internally interesting but externally unanchored signals are blocked before HardSeed birth.
Source-family documented behavior is blocked before HardSeed birth.
Known or trivial behavior is blocked before HardSeed birth.
HardSeed birth requires an explicit pre-birth domain-significance hypothesis, not only a runtime residual.
The domain-significance hypothesis must state the scientific/formal/measurement significance being tested.
The hypothesis must state what external domain interpretation, formal boundary, or measurement conclusion would change.
The hypothesis must define a novelty discriminator against known/source-family mechanisms.
The hypothesis must define a falsifier before HardSeed birth.
The domain-significance hypothesis must bind at least two public-safe external evidence refs already present in the runtime evidence set.
The domain-significance hypothesis must be tested by runtime evidence before HardSeed birth.
The domain-significance hypothesis must be supported by runtime evidence before HardSeed birth.
HardSeed birth requires an external problem anchor with public inspectability and non-internal value before runtime evidence can count.
HardSeed birth requires a tested and supported domain-significance hypothesis before runtime evidence can count.
HardSeed birth requires loaded, executed, generated, or checked runtime evidence.
Deterministic design profiles are planning evidence only; HardSeed birth requires real loaded/executed/generated target evidence.
HardSeed birth requires public-safe source and evidence refs, including a concrete public URL.
HardSeed birth is blocked when any simple baseline explains the signal.
HardSeed birth is blocked when residual magnitude is below the second-stage pressure floor of 0.1.
HardSeed birth requires at least one rival mechanism to be weakened or scoped.
HardSeed birth requires a nontrivial residual beyond metadata, tool, pipeline, or baseline success.
HardSeed birth requires cross-source or cross-slice recurrence before InsightCandidate birth is possible.
HardSeed birth is blocked when counterexamples collapse the claim.
HardSeed birth requires an independent holdout/replay path or bounded nonfatal caveat.

hardSeed.claim: Mechanism-first generator gaia_astrometric_excess_significance_generator produced a birth-eligible hard seed for cross-slice Gaia astrometric residual after source-quality and catalog-control ablations. Domain scientific significance hypothesis: An astrophysics catalog residual has domain scientific significance only when it exposes a reproducible measurement-outcome pattern across independent public sky/source slices that is not absorbed by magnitude, color, crowding, scan-law, or catalog-quality rivals. Expected domain change: Discovery-scored evidence would be a narrow public-catalog residual claim that changes interpretation of a measured astrometric outcome with cross-slice support, not an astropy or astroquery pipeline success. Novelty discriminator: The claimed signal must be not documented outside known source-family behavior and must change interpretation of the external target outcome. Candidate prediction: the astrometric residual recurs across independent sky and source slices after magnitude color crowding scan-law and catalog-quality controls. Rival prediction: magnitude color crowding scan-law source-family or catalog-quality controls erase the residual. The narrow seed says only that target gaia_astrometric_excess_significance_generator-target-01 deserves downstream InsightCandidate pressure because the runtime evidence survived simple baselines, rivals, counterexamples, cross-source support, holdout/replay availability, and the pre-birth domain-significance hypothesis gate.
hardSeed.observation: Runtime generator evidence measured cross-slice Gaia astrometric residual after source-quality and catalog-control ablations with outcome 0.4256 and residual magnitude 0.1343; this is a hard seed only, not a discovery candidate or Fund.
